_**User**_

create genre filter chips on existing @streamlist/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx 
pick mentioned colors from @streamlist/src/theme/colors.ts 
Horizontally scrollable, no scroll indicator
Active chip: secondary_container (#822625) background, on_surface text, title-sm
Inactive chip: surface_container_high background, on_surface_variant text
No borders on chips

and in the last, wire this component with api /genre/movie/list response to show on home screen

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adding genre chips UI**

I'm adding genre chips UI to HomeScreen with the specified styling. I'm implementing genre chip filtering functionality to display movies by selected genre. I'm implementing genre chip filtering with state management for selected genre ID. 

**Implementing genre chips**

I'm adjusting the genre chip implementation to fetch and display movies by genre from the API. I'm extending useHome to manage selected genre state and API calls for genre filtering. I'm adding selectedGenreId state to manage genre filtering behavior. 

I'm implementing horizontal genre chips with state management for filtering discover results. I'm refactoring useHome to support genre-based filtering by extending it to accept selectedGenreId and fetch discover data when a genre is selected. 

**Refactoring useHome**

I'm updating useHome to accept selectedGenreId parameter and refactor its return type to include discoverByGenre data. I'm adjusting useHome to show trending/top rated when no genre selected, or fetch discover data when genre is chosen. 

**Updating home screen**

I'm implementing genre filtering on the home screen by adding state for selectedGenreId and replacing Trending with filtered content when a genre is chosen. I'm adding a synthetic 'All' chip with id null to handle genre filtering and extending useHome to fetch discover content when a genre is selected. I'm refactoring useHome to handle all data fetching with conditional logic based on genre selection. 

I'm refactoring useHome to conditionally fetch discover data only when a genre filter is active, using useEffect with selectedGenreId dependency. I'm refactoring useHome to fetch discover data conditionally with a single load function using Promise.all. I'm refactoring the discover fetch to only reload when genre chips change, avoiding full page loading skeletons. I'm implementing useHome hook to manage base data and discover rows separately. I'm adding `discoverByGenre` to the `HomeScreenData` type. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating home screen**

I'm updating `useHome` to accept `selectedGenreId` and fetch discover data when it changes. I'm implementing genre filter chips and showing discover data for selected genres in a horizontal scroll view. I'm adding a 'Popular in [Genre]' section that appears below filter chips when a genre is selected, using discover data. 

I'm implementing the logic to manage genre selection state and conditional fetching of discover data. I'm modifying useHome to conditionally include discover data in load() when a genre is selected. I'm adding a secondary effect to refetch discover movies when genre changes. 

**Refactoring fetch logic**

I'm refactoring the fetch logic to only refetch discover when genre changes, avoiding unnecessary refetches of trending/top-rated. I'm extracting the base loading logic into a separate function to improve readability. I noticed a bug where `selectedGenreId` isn't accessible in the closure for `setData`, so I'll fix the closure scope. I'm simplifying the data structure for the home screen by defining a clear interface and managing state properly. 

**Managing state with hooks**

I'm initializing loading and error states with useState for better UI feedback. 

**Optimizing data fetching**

I'm refactoring the data fetching to avoid unnecessary re-fetching of trending movies when selectedGenreId changes. I'm implementing useEffect for discover only when selectedGenreId changes to reduce API calls. I'm unifying the data loading logic to ensure discover data is properly refetched when needed. I'm refactoring the data fetching logic to use concurrent requests and conditional API calls. I'll split initial data loading from genre-specific fetches to avoid unnecessary refetches. I must reload everything when switching genres to ensure data consistency. 

I'm implementing loadBase to separate initial data loading from discover data, and adding error handling for the refetch logic. 

**Handling discover errors**

I'll use setError to show error for whole home screen on discover failure, since it's simpler and aligns with existing error handling patterns. I'll catch discover errors and set discoverByGenre to empty results with optional inline error message. I'll implement error handling for discover fetch failures by setting error state and preserving previous data. I'm implementing cleanup logic to prevent state updates from cancelled requests. I'm removing `data` from dependencies to prevent circular effects and ensure fetch only runs when `selectedGenreId` changes. I'm ensuring fetchDiscover runs after base data loads to prevent incorrect data updates when genre changes before initial load completes. 

I'm fixing the discover effect to only run after base data loads to prevent incorrect updates. I'm ensuring the discover effect only runs when data is loaded and selectedGenreId is set to avoid premature fetching. I'm fixing stale closure issues by using functional setData updates to ensure latest data is read when selectedGenreId changes. I'm implementing error handling for the discover movies fetch. 

**Optimizing effects**

I'm refactoring the home screen effects to avoid wasteful refetches on genre changes. I'm extracting the base data loading into a reusable async function to avoid duplicate fetch calls. I'm fixing the genre-based data loading to prevent unnecessary re-fetches during initial load. I'm simplifying the data loading logic to use a unified approach with concurrent fetches. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Writing hook tests**

Here’s what was implemented.

### What changed

1. **`src/hooks/useHome.ts`**
   - `HomeScreenData` now includes `discoverByGenre: PaginatedResponse<MovieListItem> | null`.
   - `useHome(selectedGenreId: number | null)` loads trending, top rated, and genres in parallel; when `selectedGenreId` is set it also loads **`/discover/movie`** via existing `fetchDiscoverMoviesByGenre` so the home list matches the chosen genre.
   - Refetch (retry) reloads everything for the current genre.

2. **`src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx`**
   - `selectedGenreId` state (tap the same chip again to clear).
   - Top **horizontal** `ScrollView` with `showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}`.
   - Chips use **`colors.secondary_container`** / **`colors.surface_container_high`**, **`colors.on_surface`** / **`colors.on_surface_variant`**, **`typography.textStyle.titleSm`** for the active state; no borders.
   - Genres come from **`data.genres.genres`** (from **`/genre/movie/list`**), sorted by name.
   - When a genre is active and discover has results, a carousel is shown above **Trending** with the genre name as the section title.

3. **`src/components/skeletons/HomeScreenSkeleton.tsx`**
   - Shimmer placeholders for the chip row so loading matches the new layout.

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `streamlist/src/hooks/useHome.ts` | Genre discover wiring + `HomeScreenData` shape |
| `streamlist/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx` | Chips UI + discover row |
| `streamlist/src/components/skeletons/HomeScreenSkeleton.tsx` | Chip row skeleton |

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s how **selected genre → API → UI** is wired in your project.

### 1. **UI state: which genre is selected**

In `HomeScreen`, `selectedGenreId` is React state (`number | null`). It starts as `null` (no genre filter).

```187:192:/home/tusharsaini/CursorAI-training/Phase 2/streamlist/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx
export function HomeScreen(): React.ReactElement {
  const [selectedGenreId, setSelectedGenreId] = useState<number | null>(null);
  const home = useHome(selectedGenreId);
  const onToggleGenre = (genreId: number): void => {
    setSelectedGenreId((current: number | null) => (current === genreId ? null : genreId));
  };
```

Tapping a chip calls `onToggleGenre`: same chip again clears selection (`null`); a different chip sets that genre’s id.

That value is passed into **`useHome(selectedGenreId)`**, so the hook always knows the current selection.

---

### 2. **Data loading: genres list + discover for the selection**

`useHome` depends on `selectedGenreId` in its `load` callback:

```23:44:/home/tusharsaini/CursorAI-training/Phase 2/streamlist/src/hooks/useHome.ts
  const load = useCallback(async (): Promise<void> => {
    setLoading(true);
    setError(null);
    try {
      const [trending, topRated, genres] = await Promise.all([
        fetchTrendingMoviesWeek(1),
        fetchTopRatedMovies(1),
        fetchMovieGenres(),
      ]);
      const discoverByGenre: PaginatedResponse<MovieListItem> | null =
        selectedGenreId !== null ? await fetchDiscoverMoviesByGenre(selectedGenreId) : null;
      setData({ trending, topRated, genres, discoverByGenre });
    } catch (err: unknown) {
      setError(errorMessageFromUnknown(err));
      setData(null);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  }, [selectedGenreId]);
  useEffect(() => {
    load().catch(() => undefined);
  }, [load]);
```

- **Chip labels** come from `fetchMovieGenres()` → `/genre/movie/list`, stored as `data.genres`.
- **Movies for the selected genre** come from `fetchDiscoverMoviesByGenre(selectedGenreId)` (TMDB **discover** with `with_genres`), stored as `data.discoverByGenre`. If `selectedGenreId` is `null`, `discoverByGenre` is set to `null` and no discover request is made for that branch.

Whenever `selectedGenreId` changes, `load`’s identity changes, the `useEffect` runs again, and the hook refetches **trending, top rated, genres, and optionally discover** in one load cycle.

---

### 3. **Rendering: chips + optional discover row**

- Chips compare `selectedGenreId === genre.id` for the active style.
- The extra carousel uses `data.discoverByGenre?.results` and only renders the block when there is a selection, a resolved name, and at least one result:

```78:142:/home/tusharsaini/CursorAI-training/Phase 2/streamlist/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx
  const discoverResults: MovieListItem[] = data.discoverByGenre?.results ?? [];
  const selectedGenreName: string | undefined = data.genres.genres.find(
    (g: Genre) => g.id === selectedGenreId,
  )?.name;
  // ...
      {selectedGenreId !== null && selectedGenreName !== undefined && discoverResults.length > 0 ? (
        <>
          <Text style={styles.sectionTitle}>{selectedGenreName}</Text>
          <ScrollView
            horizontal
            ...
          >
            {discoverResults.map((item: MovieListItem) => (
```

So: **selection** drives **`useHome` → `discoverByGenre`**; **display** maps `discoverResults` to posters and uses the genre list only to resolve the **section title** (`selectedGenreName`).

---

### Summary

| Concern | Mechanism |
|--------|-----------|
| Remember selection | `useState` in `HomeScreen` |
| Pass selection into data layer | `useHome(selectedGenreId)` |
| Load movies for that genre | `fetchDiscoverMoviesByGenre(selectedGenreId)` inside `load` when id ≠ `null` |
| Show/hide carousel | Condition on `selectedGenreId`, `selectedGenreName`, and `discoverResults.length > 0` |

Note: the discover API is **not** the same as `/genre/movie/list` (that endpoint only returns genre id/name pairs). The **response of the selected genre** in terms of movies is the **discover** response stored in `discoverByGenre`.
